Not a Medium member? Use the free link below. I write these because I enjoy breaking down interview problems. If this one helped, a clap or repost lets me know it’s worth writing the next one.
[LINK]
Problem Statement
You are given a list of user accounts, where each account is represented as a list of strings. The first string is the person’s name, and the remaining strings are their email addresses.
Two accounts belong to the same person if they share at least one common email address. Even if two accounts do not directly share an email, they should still be merged if they are connected through a chain of shared emails. Every merged account should contain the person’s name followed by all unique email addresses sorted in lexicographical order. The order of the returned accounts does not matter.
Example
Input:[
["John","johnsmith@mail.com","john_newyork@mail.com"],
["John","johnsmith@mail.com","john00@mail.com"],
["Mary","mary@mail.com"],
["John","johnnybravo@mail.com"]
]
Output:[
["John","john00@mail.com","john_newyork@mail.com","johnsmith@mail.com"],
["Mary","mary@mail.com"],
["John","johnnybravo@mail.com"]
]
